entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Cultivated peanut (Arachis hypogaea L.), an important grain legume providing high-quality cooking oil, proteins, and nutrients

entities.name: SHELLING PERCENTAGE
entities.description: An important agronomic trait that significantly affects the economic value of peanut in the market

entities.name: POD YIELD
entities.description: The most important agronomic trait of peanut

entities.name: GENOMICS-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding approach using diagnostic markers to accelerate the development of improved varieties with enhanced traits

entities.name: QTL-SEQ
entities.description: A genomic approach used to identify genomic regions and candidate genes controlling specific traits

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED LINE POPULATION
entities.description: A mapping population derived from the cross of Yuanza 9102 and Xuzhou 68-4

entities.name: YUANZA 9102
entities.description: A parent variety used to construct the recombinant inbred line population

entities.name: XUZHOU 68-4
entities.description: A parent variety used to construct the recombinant inbred line population

entities.name: GENOMIC REGIONS
entities.description: Overlapped chromosomal areas identified as controlling shelling percentage

entities.name: CANDIDATE GENES
entities.description: Genes identified within genomic regions that are affected by SNPs and control shelling percentage

entities.name: SNPS
entities.description: Single nucleotide polymorphisms with non-synonymous effects or in UTRs used to identify candidate genes

entities.name: KASP
entities.description: Kompetitive Allele-Specific PCR, a cost-effective method used to develop markers for SNPs

entities.name: A09
entities.description: A chromosome containing a 2.75 Mb genomic region controlling shelling percentage

entities.name: B02
entities.description: A chromosome containing a 1.1 Mb genomic region controlling shelling percentage

entities.name: MOLECULAR BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding method utilizing diagnostic markers to achieve high shelling percentage in improved varieties

relationships.description: used to identify genomic regions controlling
